cataloging_subject.label: Religious Pieces in Prose and Verse (three-prose-piece selection)
cataloging_subject.scope_note: The intellectual/content unit delimited by the pinned U-M XML: the first three prose works in the 1914 issue of George G. Perry's EETS volume, namely Dan Jon Gaytryge's Sermon, The Mirror of Saint Edmund, and The Abbey of the Holy Ghost. This is a three-work editorial selection, not one medieval work, not the complete Perry volume, and not a Robert Thornton-authored collection. Robert Thornton copied the selected manuscript versions; work-level responsibility, dates, genres, and witness traditions remain part-specific.

catalog_summary.title: Religious Pieces in Prose and Verse (three-prose-piece selection)
catalog_summary.author: Part-specific known and anonymous authors
catalog_summary.editor: George Gresley Perry (1867); re-collated, enlarged, and revised in 1913 by Furnivall and other collaborators; U-M HTI electronic selection (1997)
catalog_summary.date: 1357 (Gaytryge); late fourteenth to early fifteenth century (Thornton Mirror version); fourteenth century (Abbey translation); copied by Thornton c. 1430-1450; electronic selection 1997
catalog_summary.region: England, with York-archdiocese and northern-English associations for Gaytryge and the Thornton copies

titles.value: Religious Pieces in Prose and Verse (three-prose-piece selection)
notes: The qualifier exposes the exact electronic boundary and avoids presenting Perry's nineteen-item volume or Thornton's manuscript as one authored work.

titles.value: Religious Pieces in Prose and Verse / edited ... by George G. Perry
notes: Exact electronic header title; the XML itself says that only the first three prose pieces are present.
Also cataloged in modern scholarship as The Lay Folks' Catechism; the Perry/U-M heading is retained as the part label.
The Thornton incipit calls it the Myrrour of Seynt Edmonde; modern scholarship also uses Speculum Religiosorum and Speculum Ecclesiae for related source states.

titles.value: The Abbey of the Holy Ghost
notes: The Thornton headings also use Religio Sancti Spiritus and Religio Munda.

language_statements.label: Middle English
notes: Latin headings, citations, and prayers occur as embedded material; Perry suppresses two Latin prayers in the Mirror where he prints their complete English equivalents.

scope.description: All three separately modeled works in the selected unit

form_statements.description: All three selected parts are encoded and printed as prose.

assertion.notes: Perry and Skeat proposed alliterative verse behind Gaytryge, and some witnesses lineate or point the text; modern scholarship cautions that it is not securely traditional alliterative verse. The selected Thornton/Perry/U-M presentation remains prose.

genre_statements.label: Edited selection
notes: This whole-scope term describes the heterogeneous electronic unit, not a medieval genre.

subject_statements.label: Interior cloister, contemplative practice, and lay devotion

summaries.value: This three-work prose selection joins John de Gaytryge's practical instruction on the creed, commandments, sacraments, works of mercy, virtues, and deadly sins; a Middle English version of Edmund of Abingdon's Mirror, guiding prayer, self-knowledge, virtue, and contemplation; and The Abbey of the Holy Ghost, an allegory that invites people unable to enter bodily religion to construct a disciplined interior cloister. The works share pastoral and devotional concerns but have separate authorship, translation histories, dates, and witness traditions.

The record stops at the three DIV1 units in ThrnReligP.xml and preserves the six nested Gaytryge divisions. It excludes Perry's preface, table of contents, marginal glosses, glossary, index, and sixteen later numbered works; those excluded materials remain relevant only as edition and editorial-practice evidence.

Robert Thornton is the scribe and compiler of the selected Lincoln Cathedral MS 91 copies, not the author of the three works. The XML source description inherits an AUTHOR element naming Thornton from the bibliographic title, while its individual incipits and modern scholarship establish distinct part-level responsibilities.

The pinned XML has three DIV1 work units, six DIV2 sections nested in Gaytryge, nineteen HEAD, fifty-four P, ten NOTE1, forty-eight manuscript-leaf MILESTONE, sixty-one PB, sixty-two EPB, and five CORR elements. The three works contain respectively 14, 29, and 7 P elements and 4, 3, and 3 notes.

extent.description: Three top-level prose works, with six nested catechetical sections in Dan Jon Gaytryge's Sermon. The electronic text spans Perry's printed pp. 1-62 and Lincoln Cathedral MS 91 ff. 197r-218v and 271r-276r in non-contiguous manuscript order.

content_parts.label: Dan Jon Gaytryge's Sermon
content_parts.summary: A six-part vernacular pastoral instruction explaining the articles of faith, commandments, sacraments, bodily works of mercy, virtues, and deadly sins for parish teaching and confession.
notes: Fourteen P and four NOTE1 elements; the other twenty-five known modern-census witnesses, including complete, reworked, and fragmentary copies, are collateral to Perry's Thornton source.

content_parts.label: The Fourteen Articles of the Creed
content_parts.summary: Explains seven articles concerning the Godhead and seven concerning Christ's humanity.

content_parts.label: The Ten Commandments
content_parts.summary: Explains duties toward God and neighbor under the ten commandments.

content_parts.label: The Seven Sacraments of Holy Church
content_parts.summary: Defines the seven sacraments and their proper reception.

content_parts.label: The Seven Bodily Works of Mercy
content_parts.summary: Lists works of mercy toward people in bodily need.

content_parts.label: The Seven Spiritual Virtues
content_parts.summary: Describes virtues that order Christian conduct and endurance.

content_parts.label: The Seven Deadly Sins
content_parts.summary: Defines and warns against pride, envy, wrath, sloth, covetousness, gluttony, and lust.

content_parts.label: The Mirror of Saint Edmund
content_parts.summary: A Middle English devotional translation in the broad Speculum Religiosorum tradition, teaching prayer, custody of the senses, virtues, self-examination, Christ's passion, and contemplation of God.
notes: Twenty-nine P and three NOTE1 elements. Perry's Latin-style chapter numbers are absent because they are editorial and do not occur in the Thornton witness.

content_parts.label: The Abbey of the Holy Ghost
content_parts.summary: A devotional allegory for people unable to enter bodily religion, organizing an interior abbey through conscience, virtues, disciplined senses, prayer, charity, and contemplative desire.
notes: Seven P and three NOTE1 elements. The selected work is The Abbey alone; the companion Charter of the Abbey of the Holy Ghost is outside the XML boundary.

related_works.title: Archbishop John Thoresby's Instruction for the People
notes: Gaytryge's English instruction belongs to Thoresby's 1357 pastoral program; the exact textual derivation from the associated Latin instruction is not forced here.

related_works.title: Speculum Religiosorum of Edmund of Abingdon
notes: Multiple Latin, Anglo-Norman, and Middle English states complicate the route to the Thornton version.

related_works.title: The Charter of the Abbey of the Holy Ghost
notes: Frequently transmitted with the Abbey but excluded from this U-M selection.

evidence.summary: Supplies the exact three-work boundary, electronic title, source and responsibility statements, headings, text, manuscript and page milestones, availability statement, and declared editorial exclusions.

evidence.citation: Element, hierarchy, page, manuscript-leaf, correction, and note audit of the pinned ThrnReligP XML.
evidence.locator: All DIV1, DIV2, HEAD, P, NOTE1, MILESTONE, PB, EPB, and CORR elements
evidence.summary: Counts 3 DIV1, 6 DIV2, 19 HEAD, 54 P, 10 NOTE1, 48 MILESTONE, 61 PB, 62 EPB, and 5 CORR; verifies the three part boundaries and Perry page ranges.

evidence.citation: ThrnReligP lineage evidence for Perry's 1867 edition, 1889 issue/reprint, 1913 revision, 1914 issue, and full scans.
evidence.summary: Supports the edition chain, exact title-page and contents facts, selection pages, editorial collaborators, explicit comparison witnesses, and revision-versus-issue chronology.

evidence.citation: ThrnReligP lineage evidence for Lincoln Cathedral MS 91 and its current official catalog.
evidence.summary: Supports Robert Thornton's scribal role, current and historical shelfmarks, manuscript date and extent, exact item and folio locators, and current catalog, onsite, reproduction, and negative public-facsimile findings.

evidence.citation: ThrnReligP lineage evidence for the Lay Folks' Catechism commission, form, and twenty-six-witness census.
evidence.summary: Supports the 1357 Gaytryge responsibility and context, modern title, form caution, complete/reworked/fragment witness census, historical shelfmarks, and exclusion of three false witnesses.

evidence.citation: ThrnReligP lineage evidence for the Mirror's source tradition, Middle English versions, witnesses, extracts, and unresolved genealogy.
evidence.summary: Supports Edmund's source authorship, the anonymous and multiply translated Middle English tradition, approximate date, witness-count and occurrence distinctions, and the need not to make Perry's parallel Anglo-Norman and Latin texts historical exemplars.

evidence.citation: ThrnReligP lineage evidence for The Abbey of the Holy Ghost, its French source, audience, witnesses, and early prints.
evidence.summary: Supports anonymous authorship and translation, fourteenth-century date, devotional-allegorical genre and lay audience, exhaustive carrier list, spurious ascriptions, and manuscript/print count tensions.

open_questions.question: Which Latin or Anglo-Norman state directly underlies the Thornton Middle English Mirror, and how should the differing counts of complete versions, manuscripts, occurrences, extracts, and fragments be reconciled?
next_steps: Consult the complete Goymer thesis and Watson chapter where access permits.
Collate the Thornton version against the full Latin, Anglo-Norman, and other Middle English version groups.
Keep Perry's Arundel 288 and Magna Bibliotheca parallels as comparison sources, not historical exemplars.

open_questions.question: How should the Abbey tradition's stated twenty-four-manuscript count be reconciled with Johnstone's twenty-five listed carriers, and the three Wynkyn de Worde editions with Thompson's older five-print count?
next_steps: Consult Consacro's complete dissertation and Buhler's incunable bibliography.
Distinguish physical carriers, duplicate occurrences, attached end fragments, and bibliographic editions before normalizing a count.

open_questions.question: Who performed the fresh whole-volume collation reported in the 1913 revision note, and who was the note's signatory J. M.?
next_steps: Consult EETS correspondence, council minutes, and production archives for OS 26 and the 1913 reprint.
Do not expand initials or assign the anonymous collation without documentary evidence.

open_questions.question: Why did U-M select exactly Perry's first three prose works?
next_steps: Seek legacy HTI project documentation if the rationale becomes catalogically important; retain the explicit XML boundary without speculation.

notes: Witness dates, holdings, shelfmarks, digital access, and image rights are modeled in the lineage manifest and do not become whole-selection descriptive properties.

The part-level witness censuses are collateral research layers. Perry's selected readings and U-M's text trace only through Lincoln Cathedral MS 91 except where Perry explicitly prints or discusses comparisons.
The date phrase 're-collated with the MS., enlarged and revised 1913' describes revision work; the physical source issue is dated 1914 and also styled 1914 (for 1913).
Content-part item counts are non-overlapping: the three work parents each count one item, while the six nested Gaytryge sections count zero additional works.
